behavior_summary: This script implements game logic for the right-leg body-part item/process. It supports being 'used' (attached/added) and responding to being 'hit' (removed/converted to a replacement visual/process).

U8P_LEGRIGHT::use marks the target object with an info key (0x0207), excludes it from some processing list, and spawns a BODYPART process (ID 0x0270) attached to that object — effectively adding/attaching the right-leg part.

U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit marks the object with a different info key (0x020B), excludes it, invokes a cleanup/free routine (FREE::2A56 with info id 0x0202), then queries how many processes of type 0x020A remain on the object. If none remain, it spawns a different BODYPART (ID 0x0431) — likely a detached or replacement visual.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LEGRIGHT::use: set-info(0x0207) -> process-exclude -> spawn(BODYPART::0270, target, 0) -> store spawn retval -> return (confidence: high).

U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit: set-info(0x020B) -> process-exclude -> push info 0x0202 + call FREE::2A56(target) -> Kernel::getNumProcesses(target, ProcessType=0x020A) -> if retval == 0 then spawn(BODYPART::0431, target, 0) -> store spawn retval -> return (confidence: high).
Decisions in hit() are driven by Kernel::getNumProcesses result; spawning of 0x0431 only happens when the checked process-type count is zero (confidence: high).
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of info IDs 0x0207, 0x020B and 0x0202 (they are inferred metadata/state keys but their gameplay semantics are unknown).
Precise effect and rules of 'process exclude' in this engine variant (what behaviors/processing are prevented).
What entity/process type is represented by process type 0x020A (which Kernel::getNumProcesses queries) and how it relates to body-part lifecycle.
Exact behavior of FREE::2A56 — inferred as cleanup/freeing but its side effects and whether it modifies other global state are uncertain.

main_function.name: U8P_LEGRIGHT::use
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_LEGRIGHT::use' activation of the right-leg item: set item/process state and attach the right-leg body-part process (BODYPART::0270) to the target object.
entry_conditions: Event 1 (U8P_LEGRIGHT::use) invoked when the LEGRIGHT object is used/activated by an actor or script.

function_map.name: U8P_LEGRIGHT::use
function_map.role: Attach right-leg body-part to a target object (set info, exclude, spawn BODYPART::0270).

function_map.name: U8P_LEGRIGHT::hit
function_map.role: Respond to hits on the right-leg: mark/exclude, perform cleanup, check remaining related processes, and spawn replacement/detached body-part (BODYPART::0431) if appropriate.

function_map.name: spawn (BODYPART::0270)
function_map.role: Engine call to create/attach BODYPART process ID 0x0270 to the target (used by U8P_LEGRIGHT::use()).

function_map.name: spawn (BODYPART::0431)
function_map.role: Engine call to create/attach BODYPART process ID 0x0431 to the target (used by hit() when no other processes remain).

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Cleanup/free routine invoked during hit(); appears to perform deallocation or state cleanup for the object/process (inferred).

function_map.name: Kernel::getNumProcesses
function_map.role: Query the number of active processes of a given ProcessType on the target object; used to decide whether to spawn 0x0431 in hit().

function_map.name: U8P_LEGRIGHT::set info
function_map.role: Engine instruction to set metadata/state info on the target object (IDs 0x0207 in U8P_LEGRIGHT::use(), 0x020B and 0x0202 in hit()).

function_map.name: U8P_LEGRIGHT::process exclude
function_map.role: Engine instruction to exclude/mark the process or object from further normal processing; used in both U8P_LEGRIGHT::use() and hit().

external_calls.purpose: Set info id 0x0207 on the target object before attaching the body part (marking state).
Exclude the process/object from some processing list after setting info (likely to prevent duplicate handling).
Attach/create the right-leg body-part process (ID 0x0270) on the target object; spawn return value stored locally.
Set info id 0x020B on the target object to record being hit/changed before cleanup.
Exclude the process/object after being hit to prevent further processing.
Perform cleanup/freeing related to the object/process (passed info id 0x0202 and target) — inferred behavior.
Check how many processes of type 0x020A remain on the target to decide whether to spawn replacement/detached part.
If Kernel::getNumProcesses returns 0 for type 0x020A, spawn BODYPART::0431 (likely a detached visual or replacement).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements game logic for the right-leg body-part item/process. It supports being 'used' (attached/added) and responding to being 'hit' (removed/converted to a replacement visual/process).
